body.title: [sprint] Strip sudo_relay out of igor/tools — the rack device owns it; Igor reaches it over the bus
body.intention: I intend that Igor contains only cognition+memory, so infrastructure he merely USES (an OS sudo relay) lives in its own rack device, not duplicated inside his tree.
body.description:
**Problem:** `sudo_relay` exists TWICE: a proper rack device `unseen_university/devices/sudo_relay/` (SudoRelayDevice/Shim wrapping the `./sudo_relay` daemon CLI) AND a 176-line copy inside Igor's tree, `igor/tools/sudo_relay.py`, registered as an Igor tool via `igor/tools/__init__.py:36`. An OS privilege-escalation relay is INFRASTRUCTURE, not cognition — it violates 'Igor uses systems, does not contain them' ([[feedback_igor_uses_not_contains]]) and the igor-strip cleanup ([[project_igor_reset_cleanup_before_codex_tickets]]). Grep confirms NOTHING imports the Igor copy except the `__init__` export — removal is low-risk.
**Proposed shape:** Delete `igor/tools/sudo_relay.py` and remove its line from `igor/tools/__init__.py` (line ~36). If Igor cognition ever needs sudo-relay, it requests it from the SudoRelay rack device over the bus (request/response envelope), NOT via an in-tree tool that pokes the relay dir directly. Do NOT re-add the capability under igor. The rack device + repo-root `./sudo_relay` daemon remain the sole owners.
**Affected files:** DELETE unseen_university/devices/igor/tools/sudo_relay.py; EDIT unseen_university/devices/igor/tools/__init__.py.
**Design rules:** Igor = cognition+memory only; infrastructure lives in its own device; preserve the rack device untouched; no TheIgors imports.
**Scope boundary:** remove the Igor-side duplicate + export only; do NOT modify the rack device or the daemon CLI; do NOT wire a new bus path in this ticket (that's future if/when Igor needs it).
**Completion criteria:** `igor/tools/sudo_relay.py` gone; grep `igor.tools.sudo_relay` / `from .sudo_relay import` / the `sudo_relay,` export → 0 live refs; `import unseen_university.devices.igor.tools` is green; the rack device `devices/sudo_relay/` still imports + self-tests green.
**Test plan:** grep-gate red→green on the igor copy + export; igor tools package import-green; sudo_relay rack-device import/self_test green.

[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
